mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 0.5 percentage points within three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment remains below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate revenue growth, generating positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 60% of observations, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the industrial production index reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks that disrupt manufacturing output and trigger short-term equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat during industrial production declines exceeding 1%, or the signal reverses direction during liquidity-driven market rallies.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation measured by CPI exceeds 3.0%, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening that raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in over 65% of high-inflation months, or the Federal Reserve explicitly rules out rate hikes despite CPI crossing the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rates signals labor market tightening that boosts wage growth and consumer confidence, supporting positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than half of observed months, or wage stagnation decouples employment gains from equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in housing starts reflect strong household balance sheets and construction sector activity that propagate through supply chains to lift corporate earnings and equity prices.
failure_condition: Housing starts growth shows zero correlation with 60-day S&P 500 returns over a 5-year sample, or construction sector downturns fail to transmit to broader equity markets.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid industrial production growth combined with stable inflation indicates healthy capacity utilization that supports manufacturing margins and triggers short-term equity buying.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns turn negative during periods of strong industrial output, or inflation spikes simultaneously negate the positive supply signal.
